flowSpec.goal: Execute a full Software Development Lifecycle with heavy QA to build a marketing website for CloudPulse, a cloud monitoring SaaS platform. Follow all phases in order: requirements, design, task planning, implementation, E2E testing, Lighthouse audit, code quality analysis, visual snapshots, and iterative review. Produce a single index.html file that passes all quality gates including Playwright E2E tests and Lighthouse audits.

source.text: "You are a brand strategist. Generate comprehensive brand guidelines for CloudPulse, a cloud monitoring SaaS platform. Include: company mission (real-time cloud infrastructure visibility), primary color

position.text: let brand = prompt "You are a brand strategist. Generate comprehensive brand guidelines for CloudPulse, a cloud monitoring SaaS platform. Include: company mission (real-time cloud infrastructure visibility), primary color #2563EB (electric blue), secondary color #1E40AF (deep blue), accent color #10B981 (emerald green for success states), neutral palette (slate grays), typography recommendations (modern sans-serif like Inter or system fonts), tone of voice (professional but approachable, technically credible), logo usage rules, spacing/layout principles. Format as a structured markdown document."
run: echo '${brand}' > brand.md

source.text: "You are a product manager. Generate a detailed requirements document for the CloudPulse marketing website. The site must be a single index.html file with inline CSS (no external dependencies). Required sections: (1) Navigation bar with logo and anchor links, (2) Hero section with headline, subheadline, and CTA button, (3) Features section with at least 6 feature cards covering real-time monitoring, alerting, dashboards, multi-cloud support, team collaboration, and API access, (4) Pricing section with 3 tiers (Starter/Pro/Enterprise), (5) Testimonials section with at least 3 customer quotes, (6) Integrations section showing supported cloud providers (AWS, Azure, GCP), (7) FAQ section with at least 5 questions using accordion pattern (details/summary or JS toggle), (8) Footer with CTA, links, and copyright. Acceptance criteria: responsive design (mobile-first), semantic HTML5, accessible (WCAG 2.1 AA), brand color

position.text: let requirements = prompt "You are a product manager. Generate a detailed requirements document for the CloudPulse marketing website. The site must be a single index.html file with inline CSS (no external dependencies). Required sections: (1) Navigation bar with logo and anchor links, (2) Hero section with headline, subheadline, and CTA button, (3) Features section with at least 6 feature cards covering real-time monitoring, alerting, dashboards, multi-cloud support, team collaboration, and API access, (4) Pricing section with 3 tiers (Starter/Pro/Enterprise), (5) Testimonials section with at least 3 customer quotes, (6) Integrations section showing supported cloud providers (AWS, Azure, GCP), (7) FAQ section with at least 5 questions using accordion pattern (details/summary or JS toggle), (8) Footer with CTA, links, and copyright. Acceptance criteria: responsive design (mobile-first), semantic HTML5, accessible (WCAG 2.1 AA), brand color #2563EB used throughout, minimum 5000 characters total, professional production quality. Format as structured markdown with numbered acceptance criteria."
run: echo '${requirements}' > requirements.md

source.text: You are a UI designer. Using the brand guidelines in brand.md and requirements in requirements.md, create a detailed component design document for the CloudPulse marketing website. For each section (nav, hero, features, pricing, testimonials, integrations, FAQ, footer), specify: HTML structure (semantic elements to use), CSS approach (flexbox/grid layouts, responsive breakpoints at 768px and 1024px), color application (which brand colors where), spacing (consistent padding/margin system using 8px grid), typography (font sizes, weights, line heights), interactive states (hover effects on buttons and cards), and visual hierarchy. IMPORTANT: The FAQ section MUST use clickable accordion behavior — either HTML details/summary elements or JavaScript click handlers that toggle visibility. Specify the exact interaction: click question to expand answer, click again to collapse. Also specify the overall page structure: max-width container (1200px), section spacing, and the responsive mobile menu approach. Format as structured markdown.

position.text: let design = prompt "You are a UI designer. Using the brand guidelines in brand.md and requirements in requirements.md, create a detailed component design document for the CloudPulse marketing website. For each section (nav, hero, features, pricing, testimonials, integrations, FAQ, footer), specify: HTML structure (semantic elements to use), CSS approach (flexbox/grid layouts, responsive breakpoints at 768px and 1024px), color application (which brand colors where), spacing (consistent padding/margin system using 8px grid), typography (font sizes, weights, line heights), interactive states (hover effects on buttons and cards), and visual hierarchy. IMPORTANT: The FAQ section MUST use clickable accordion behavior — either HTML details/summary elements or JavaScript click handlers that toggle visibility. Specify the exact interaction: click question to expand answer, click again to collapse. Also specify the overall page structure: max-width container (1200px), section spacing, and the responsive mobile menu approach. Format as structured markdown."
run: echo '${design}' > design.md

source.text: You are a technical lead. Based on the requirements in requirements.md and design in design.md, generate a numbered task list for building the CloudPulse marketing website. Each task should be a single implementable unit. Use this exact format — one task per line, numbered: 1. Create base HTML structure with doctype head and meta tags 2. Build navigation bar with logo and anchor links 3. Build hero section with headline subheadline and CTA 4. Build features section with 6 feature cards in responsive grid 5. Build pricing section with 3 tier cards 6. Build testimonials section with customer quotes 7. Build integrations section with cloud provider logos 8. Build FAQ section with accordion behavior using details/summary or JS click handlers 9. Build footer with CTA links and copyright 10. Add responsive CSS for mobile breakpoints at 375px 768px and 1024px 11. Add hover effects and transitions 12. Add accessibility attributes and skip navigation. Output ONLY the numbered list, no other text.

position.text: let tasks = prompt "You are a technical lead. Based on the requirements in requirements.md and design in design.md, generate a numbered task list for building the CloudPulse marketing website. Each task should be a single implementable unit. Use this exact format — one task per line, numbered: 1. Create base HTML structure with doctype head and meta tags 2. Build navigation bar with logo and anchor links 3. Build hero section with headline subheadline and CTA 4. Build features section with 6 feature cards in responsive grid 5. Build pricing section with 3 tier cards 6. Build testimonials section with customer quotes 7. Build integrations section with cloud provider logos 8. Build FAQ section with accordion behavior using details/summary or JS click handlers 9. Build footer with CTA links and copyright 10. Add responsive CSS for mobile breakpoints at 375px 768px and 1024px 11. Add hover effects and transitions 12. Add accessibility attributes and skip navigation. Output ONLY the numbered list, no other text."
run: echo '${tasks}' > tasks.md

body.text: You are a senior frontend developer. Implement this task for the CloudPulse marketing website: ${task}. Read the current index.html if it exists and ADD to it (do not overwrite previous work). Follow the brand guidelines in brand.md (primary color

position.text: prompt: You are a senior frontend developer. Implement this task for the CloudPulse marketing website: ${task}. Read the current index.html if it exists and ADD to it (do not overwrite previous work). Follow the brand guidelines in brand.md (primary color #2563EB, secondary #1E40AF, accent #10B981) and the component design in design.md. Write all code to index.html as a single self-contained file with inline CSS in a style tag and any JavaScript in a script tag at the end of body. Use semantic HTML5, ensure responsive design, and include ARIA attributes for accessibility. If index.html does not exist yet, create it with the full HTML5 boilerplate including viewport meta tag, charset, and title "CloudPulse - Cloud Monitoring Made Simple". IMPORTANT: The FAQ section MUST have clickable accordion behavior — use details/summary HTML elements or add JavaScript click handlers that toggle answer visibility with a class toggle.
foreach task in ${tasks}

nodes.command: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); if(!fs.existsSync('index.html')){process.exit(1)} const h=fs.readFileSync('index.html','utf8'); if(h.length<500){process.exit(1)} console.log('index.html exists: '+h.length+' chars')"

position.text: run: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); if(!fs.existsSync('index.html')){process.exit(1)} const h=fs.readFileSync('index.html','utf8'); if(h.length<500){process.exit(1)} console.log('index.html exists: '+h.length+' chars')"

thenBranch.text: index.html is missing or too small. Create a complete CloudPulse marketing website in index.html with all required sections (nav, hero, features, pricing, testimonials, integrations, FAQ with accordion behavior, footer). Use brand color

position.text: prompt: index.html is missing or too small. Create a complete CloudPulse marketing website in index.html with all required sections (nav, hero, features, pricing, testimonials, integrations, FAQ with accordion behavior, footer). Use brand color #2563EB. Make it a single self-contained HTML file with inline CSS and JavaScript. It must be at least 5000 characters and include responsive design. The FAQ MUST use details/summary elements or JavaScript click handlers for accordion open/close behavior.
if command_failed

source.text: Write a Playwright E2E test file (e2e.spec.js) for the CloudPulse website at index.html. Use @playwright/test. The tests must cover: (1) page loads and title contains 'CloudPulse', (2) all nav links exist and have valid href anchors pointing to sections, (3) all 3 pricing tiers are visible (Starter, Pro, Enterprise), (4) FAQ accordion opens/closes on click — find clickable FAQ elements (details/summary or elements with click handlers), click one, verify answer becomes visible, click again, verify it collapses, (5) CTA buttons are present and clickable (at least 2 buttons with text like 'Get Started' or 'Start Free Trial'), (6) responsive layout: test at viewport widths 375px, 768px, and 1024px — verify hero section is visible at each width and no horizontal overflow, (7) footer contains copyright text and at least one link. Use file:// protocol to load index.html: const url = 'file://' + require('path').resolve('index.html'). Save the test file to e2e.spec.js. Make tests resilient — use data-testid or semantic selectors, add reasonable timeouts.

position.text: let e2e_tests = prompt "Write a Playwright E2E test file (e2e.spec.js) for the CloudPulse website at index.html. Use @playwright/test. The tests must cover: (1) page loads and title contains 'CloudPulse', (2) all nav links exist and have valid href anchors pointing to sections, (3) all 3 pricing tiers are visible (Starter, Pro, Enterprise), (4) FAQ accordion opens/closes on click — find clickable FAQ elements (details/summary or elements with click handlers), click one, verify answer becomes visible, click again, verify it collapses, (5) CTA buttons are present and clickable (at least 2 buttons with text like 'Get Started' or 'Start Free Trial'), (6) responsive layout: test at viewport widths 375px, 768px, and 1024px — verify hero section is visible at each width and no horizontal overflow, (7) footer contains copyright text and at least one link. Use file:// protocol to load index.html: const url = 'file://' + require('path').resolve('index.html'). Save the test file to e2e.spec.js. Make tests resilient — use data-testid or semantic selectors, add reasonable timeouts."
run: npm init -y 2>/dev/null; npm install --save-dev @playwright/test 2>&1 | tail -5
run: npx playwright install chromium 2>&1 | tail -3

nodes.command: node -e "require('fs').writeFileSync('playwright.config.js', \"const { defineConfig } = require('@playwright/test');\\nmodule.exports = defineConfig({\\n  testDir: '.',\\n  testMatch: 'e2e.spec.js',\\n  use: {\\n    browserName: 'chromium',\\n    headless: true,\\n    screenshot: 'only-on-failure',\\n  },\\n  retries: 0,\\n  reporter: [['list'], ['json', { outputFile: 'test-results.json' }]],\\n});\\n\")"

position.text: run: node -e "require('fs').writeFileSync('playwright.config.js', \"const { defineConfig } = require('@playwright/test');\\nmodule.exports = defineConfig({\\n  testDir: '.',\\n  testMatch: 'e2e.spec.js',\\n  use: {\\n    browserName: 'chromium',\\n    headless: true,\\n    screenshot: 'only-on-failure',\\n  },\\n  retries: 0,\\n  reporter: [['list'], ['json', { outputFile: 'test-results.json' }]],\\n});\\n\")"
run: npx playwright test e2e.spec.js --config=playwright.config.js 2>&1 | tail -30

source.command: cat test-results.json 2>/dev/null | node -e \"const d=JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('/dev/stdin','utf8')); const fails=d.suites?.[0]?.specs?.filter(s=>s.tests?.[0]?.results?.[0]?.status!=='passed')?.map(s=>s.title+': '+s.tests[0].results[0].error?.message?.split('\\n')[0])||[]; console.log(fails.length?fails.join('\\n'):'See test output above')\" 2>/dev/null || echo 'Test run failed — check e2e.spec.js for syntax errors'

position.text: let test_output = run "cat test-results.json 2>/dev/null | node -e \"const d=JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('/dev/stdin','utf8')); const fails=d.suites?.[0]?.specs?.filter(s=>s.tests?.[0]?.results?.[0]?.status!=='passed')?.map(s=>s.title+': '+s.tests[0].results[0].error?.message?.split('\\n')[0])||[]; console.log(fails.length?fails.join('\\n'):'See test output above')\" 2>/dev/null || echo 'Test run failed — check e2e.spec.js for syntax errors'"

thenBranch.text: The Playwright E2E tests failed. Here are the failures: ${test_output}. Read both e2e.spec.js and index.html. Determine whether the test expectations are wrong or the HTML needs fixing. Fix whichever is the root cause — prefer fixing index.html if it is genuinely missing behavior (like accordion toggle), but fix e2e.spec.js if the selectors are wrong. Keep both files consistent. Do NOT delete tests, only fix them.

position.text: prompt: The Playwright E2E tests failed. Here are the failures: ${test_output}. Read both e2e.spec.js and index.html. Determine whether the test expectations are wrong or the HTML needs fixing. Fix whichever is the root cause — prefer fixing index.html if it is genuinely missing behavior (like accordion toggle), but fix e2e.spec.js if the selectors are wrong. Keep both files consistent. Do NOT delete tests, only fix them.
if command_failed
retry max 3

source.command: node -e \"try{const d=JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('test-results.json','utf8')); const specs=d.suites?.[0]?.specs||[]; const passed=specs.filter(s=>s.tests?.[0]?.results?.[0]?.status==='passed').length; console.log(passed+'/'+specs.length+' passed')}catch(e){console.log('no results')}\"

position.text: let e2e_result = run "node -e \"try{const d=JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('test-results.json','utf8')); const specs=d.suites?.[0]?.specs||[]; const passed=specs.filter(s=>s.tests?.[0]?.results?.[0]?.status==='passed').length; console.log(passed+'/'+specs.length+' passed')}catch(e){console.log('no results')}\""
run: npm install --save-dev lighthouse 2>&1 | tail -3

source.command: node -e \"const lh=require('lighthouse'); const chromeLauncher=require('lighthouse/chrome-launcher'); (async()=>{const chrome=await chromeLauncher.launch({chromeFlags:['--headless','--no-sandbox','--disable-gpu']}); const result=await lh('file://'+require('path').resolve('index.html'),{port:chrome.port,output:'json',onlyCategories:['performance','accessibility','best-practices','seo']}); await chrome.kill(); const c=result.lhr.categories; const scores={perf:Math.round(c.performance.score*100),a11y:Math.round(c.accessibility.score*100),bp:Math.round(c['best-practices'].score*100),seo:Math.round(c.seo.score*100)}; require('fs').writeFileSync('lighthouse-report.json',JSON.stringify(scores,null,2)); console.log(JSON.stringify(scores))})().catch(e=>{console.log(JSON.stringify({perf:-1,a11y:-1,bp:-1,seo:-1,error:e.message}))})\" 2>/dev/null

position.text: let lighthouse_raw = run "node -e \"const lh=require('lighthouse'); const chromeLauncher=require('lighthouse/chrome-launcher'); (async()=>{const chrome=await chromeLauncher.launch({chromeFlags:['--headless','--no-sandbox','--disable-gpu']}); const result=await lh('file://'+require('path').resolve('index.html'),{port:chrome.port,output:'json',onlyCategories:['performance','accessibility','best-practices','seo']}); await chrome.kill(); const c=result.lhr.categories; const scores={perf:Math.round(c.performance.score*100),a11y:Math.round(c.accessibility.score*100),bp:Math.round(c['best-practices'].score*100),seo:Math.round(c.seo.score*100)}; require('fs').writeFileSync('lighthouse-report.json',JSON.stringify(scores,null,2)); console.log(JSON.stringify(scores))})().catch(e=>{console.log(JSON.stringify({perf:-1,a11y:-1,bp:-1,seo:-1,error:e.message}))})\" 2>/dev/null"

thenBranch.command: echo '{"perf":"N/A","a11y":"N/A","bp":"N/A","seo":"N/A","note":"Lighthouse requires Chrome and may not run in all environments"}' > lighthouse-report.json

position.text: run: echo '{"perf":"N/A","a11y":"N/A","bp":"N/A","seo":"N/A","note":"Lighthouse requires Chrome and may not run in all environments"}' > lighthouse-report.json
if ${lighthouse_raw} contains "error"

let lh_needs_fix = run "node -e \"try{const s=JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('lighthouse-report.json','utf8')); const low=[]; if(s.a11y>=0&&s.a11y<80)low.push('accessibility:'+s.a11y); if(s.seo>=0&&s.seo<80)low.push('seo:'+s.seo); if(s.bp>=0&&s.bp<80)low.push('best-practices:'+s.bp); console.log(low.length?'FIX:'+low.join(','):'PASS')}catch(e){console.log('SKIP')}\""

thenBranch.text: Lighthouse audit found low scores: ${lh_needs_fix}. Read index.html and improve: for accessibility — add alt text, aria-labels, sufficient color contrast, form labels; for SEO — add meta description, proper heading hierarchy, canonical link; for best practices — use HTTPS links, add doctype, avoid deprecated APIs. Make targeted fixes without removing existing content.

position.text: prompt: Lighthouse audit found low scores: ${lh_needs_fix}. Read index.html and improve: for accessibility — add alt text, aria-labels, sufficient color contrast, form labels; for SEO — add meta description, proper heading hierarchy, canonical link; for best practices — use HTTPS links, add doctype, avoid deprecated APIs. Make targeted fixes without removing existing content.
if ${lh_needs_fix} contains "FIX"

source.command: node -e \"const h=require('fs').readFileSync('index.html','utf8'); const scripts=(h.match(/<script[^>]*>([\\s\\S]*?)<\\/script>/gi)||[]); const totalJS=scripts.map(s=>s.replace(/<\\/?script[^>]*>/gi,'')).join('\\n'); const ifs=(totalJS.match(/\\bif\\b|\\belse\\b|\\bfor\\b|\\bwhile\\b|\\bswitch\\b|\\bcase\\b|\\bcatch\\b|\\?/g)||[]).length; const funcs=(totalJS.match(/function\\b|=>/g)||[]).length; const lines=totalJS.split('\\n').filter(l=>l.trim()).length; const result={jsBytes:totalJS.length,jsLines:lines,branches:ifs,functions:funcs,avgComplexityPerFn:funcs>0?Math.round(ifs/funcs*10)/10:0}; require('fs').writeFileSync('complexity-report.json',JSON.stringify(result,null,2)); console.log(JSON.stringify(result))\"

position.text: let js_complexity = run "node -e \"const h=require('fs').readFileSync('index.html','utf8'); const scripts=(h.match(/<script[^>]*>([\\s\\S]*?)<\\/script>/gi)||[]); const totalJS=scripts.map(s=>s.replace(/<\\/?script[^>]*>/gi,'')).join('\\n'); const ifs=(totalJS.match(/\\bif\\b|\\belse\\b|\\bfor\\b|\\bwhile\\b|\\bswitch\\b|\\bcase\\b|\\bcatch\\b|\\?/g)||[]).length; const funcs=(totalJS.match(/function\\b|=>/g)||[]).length; const lines=totalJS.split('\\n').filter(l=>l.trim()).length; const result={jsBytes:totalJS.length,jsLines:lines,branches:ifs,functions:funcs,avgComplexityPerFn:funcs>0?Math.round(ifs/funcs*10)/10:0}; require('fs').writeFileSync('complexity-report.json',JSON.stringify(result,null,2)); console.log(JSON.stringify(result))\""

source.command: node -e \"const h=require('fs').readFileSync('index.html','utf8'); const styles=(h.match(/<style[^>]*>([\\s\\S]*?)<\\/style>/gi)||[]); const totalCSS=styles.map(s=>s.replace(/<\\/?style[^>]*>/gi,'')).join('\\n'); const selectors=(totalCSS.match(/[^{}]+(?=\\{)/g)||[]).length; const mediaQueries=(totalCSS.match(/@media/g)||[]).length; const result={cssBytes:totalCSS.length,selectors:selectors,mediaQueries:mediaQueries}; console.log(JSON.stringify(result))\"

position.text: let css_metrics = run "node -e \"const h=require('fs').readFileSync('index.html','utf8'); const styles=(h.match(/<style[^>]*>([\\s\\S]*?)<\\/style>/gi)||[]); const totalCSS=styles.map(s=>s.replace(/<\\/?style[^>]*>/gi,'')).join('\\n'); const selectors=(totalCSS.match(/[^{}]+(?=\\{)/g)||[]).length; const mediaQueries=(totalCSS.match(/@media/g)||[]).length; const result={cssBytes:totalCSS.length,selectors:selectors,mediaQueries:mediaQueries}; console.log(JSON.stringify(result))\""

source.command: node -e \"try{const c=JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('complexity-report.json','utf8')); if(c.avgComplexityPerFn>10){console.log('HIGH: avg complexity '+c.avgComplexityPerFn+' per function')}else if(c.jsLines>200){console.log('LARGE: '+c.jsLines+' lines of inline JS')}else{console.log('OK')}}catch(e){console.log('OK')}\"

position.text: let complexity_warning = run "node -e \"try{const c=JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('complexity-report.json','utf8')); if(c.avgComplexityPerFn>10){console.log('HIGH: avg complexity '+c.avgComplexityPerFn+' per function')}else if(c.jsLines>200){console.log('LARGE: '+c.jsLines+' lines of inline JS')}else{console.log('OK')}}catch(e){console.log('OK')}\""

thenBranch.text: The inline JavaScript in index.html has high cyclomatic complexity (${complexity_warning}). Read index.html and simplify the JavaScript: extract repeated logic into functions, reduce nested conditionals, use early returns. Keep all functionality working.

position.text: prompt: The inline JavaScript in index.html has high cyclomatic complexity (${complexity_warning}). Read index.html and simplify the JavaScript: extract repeated logic into functions, reduce nested conditionals, use early returns. Keep all functionality working.
if ${complexity_warning} contains "HIGH"

nodes.command: node -e "const { chromium } = require('playwright'); (async()=>{const b=await chromium.launch({args:['--no-sandbox']}); const ctx=await b.newContext({viewport:{width:1280,height:720}}); const p=await ctx.newPage(); await p.goto('file://'+process.cwd().replace(/\\\\/g,'/')+'/index.html'); await p.waitForLoadState('networkidle'); await p.screenshot({path:'screenshot-desktop.png',fullPage:true}); console.log('Desktop screenshot saved'); await p.setViewportSize({width:768,height:1024}); await p.screenshot({path:'screenshot-tablet.png',fullPage:true}); console.log('Tablet screenshot saved'); await p.setViewportSize({width:375,height:812}); await p.screenshot({path:'screenshot-mobile.png',fullPage:true}); console.log('Mobile screenshot saved'); await b.close();})()" 2>&1

position.text: run: node -e "const { chromium } = require('playwright'); (async()=>{const b=await chromium.launch({args:['--no-sandbox']}); const ctx=await b.newContext({viewport:{width:1280,height:720}}); const p=await ctx.newPage(); await p.goto('file://'+process.cwd().replace(/\\\\/g,'/')+'/index.html'); await p.waitForLoadState('networkidle'); await p.screenshot({path:'screenshot-desktop.png',fullPage:true}); console.log('Desktop screenshot saved'); await p.setViewportSize({width:768,height:1024}); await p.screenshot({path:'screenshot-tablet.png',fullPage:true}); console.log('Tablet screenshot saved'); await p.setViewportSize({width:375,height:812}); await p.screenshot({path:'screenshot-mobile.png',fullPage:true}); console.log('Mobile screenshot saved'); await b.close();})()" 2>&1

source.command: node -e \"const fs=require('fs'); const files=['screenshot-desktop.png','screenshot-tablet.png','screenshot-mobile.png']; const found=files.filter(f=>fs.existsSync(f)); console.log(found.length+'/'+files.length+' screenshots captured')\"

position.text: let screenshots_ok = run "node -e \"const fs=require('fs'); const files=['screenshot-desktop.png','screenshot-tablet.png','screenshot-mobile.png']; const found=files.filter(f=>fs.existsSync(f)); console.log(found.length+'/'+files.length+' screenshots captured')\""

let qa_structure = run "node -e \"const h=require('fs').readFileSync('index.html','utf8'); const checks={html:h.includes('</html>'),head:h.includes('</head>'),body:h.includes('</body>'),nav:h.includes('<nav'),hero:h.includes('hero'),features:h.includes('feature'),pricing:h.includes('pric'),testimonials:h.includes('testimonial'),faq:h.includes('faq')||h.includes('FAQ'),footer:h.includes('<footer'),length:h.length}; const fails=Object.entries(checks).filter(([k,v])=>!v).map(([k])=>k); console.log(fails.length===0?'ALL_PASS':'FAIL: '+fails.join(', '))\""

let qa_a11y = run "node -e \"const h=require('fs').readFileSync('index.html','utf8'); const checks={viewport:h.includes('viewport'),lang:h.includes('lang='),h1:h.includes('<h1'),alt_tags:(h.match(/alt=/g)||[]).length>=1,aria:(h.match(/aria-/g)||[]).length>=1,semantic_nav:h.includes('<nav'),semantic_main:h.includes('<main')||h.includes('<section'),semantic_footer:h.includes('<footer')}; const fails=Object.entries(checks).filter(([k,v])=>!v).map(([k])=>k); console.log(fails.length===0?'ALL_PASS':'FAIL: '+fails.join(', '))\""

let qa_seo = run "node -e \"const h=require('fs').readFileSync('index.html','utf8'); const checks={title:h.includes('<title'),meta_desc:h.includes('meta')&&h.includes('description'),charset:h.includes('charset'),h1:h.includes('<h1'),h2:(h.match(/<h2/g)||[]).length>=3}; const fails=Object.entries(checks).filter(([k,v])=>!v).map(([k])=>k); console.log(fails.length===0?'ALL_PASS':'FAIL: '+fails.join(', '))\""

let qa_brand = run "node -e \"const h=require('fs').readFileSync('index.html','utf8'); const checks={name:h.includes('CloudPulse'),primary_color:h.includes('#2563EB')||h.includes('#2563eb'),has_cta:h.includes('button')||h.includes('btn'),min_length:h.length>5000}; const fails=Object.entries(checks).filter(([k,v])=>!v).map(([k])=>k); console.log(fails.length===0?'ALL_PASS':'FAIL: '+fails.join(', '))\""

run: node -e "const sections=['# QA Report — Heavy QA Variant','','## Structural Checks','${qa_structure}','','## Accessibility Checks','${qa_a11y}','','## SEO Checks','${qa_seo}','','## Brand Compliance','${qa_brand}','','## E2E Tests (Playwright)','${e2e_result}','','## Lighthouse Scores','${lighthouse_raw}','','## Code Complexity','${js_complexity}','','## CSS Metrics','${css_metrics}','','## Visual Snapshots','${screenshots_ok}']; require('fs').writeFileSync('qa-report.md',sections.join('\n')+'\n'); console.log('QA report written')"

thenBranch.text: The structure check found issues: ${qa_structure}. Read index.html and fix these structural problems. Ensure all required sections are present with proper HTML elements. Do not remove existing content.

position.text: prompt: The structure check found issues: ${qa_structure}. Read index.html and fix these structural problems. Ensure all required sections are present with proper HTML elements. Do not remove existing content.
if ${qa_structure} != "ALL_PASS"

thenBranch.text: The accessibility check found issues: ${qa_a11y}. Read index.html and fix these accessibility problems. Add missing ARIA attributes, alt text, semantic elements, and viewport meta tag as needed. Do not remove existing content.

if ${qa_a11y} != "ALL_PASS"

thenBranch.text: The SEO check found issues: ${qa_seo}. Read index.html and fix these SEO problems. Add missing title, meta description, charset, heading hierarchy as needed. Do not remove existing content.

thenBranch.text: The brand compliance check found issues: ${qa_brand}. Read index.html and fix these brand issues. Ensure "CloudPulse" appears in the content, the primary brand color

position.text: prompt: The brand compliance check found issues: ${qa_brand}. Read index.html and fix these brand issues. Ensure "CloudPulse" appears in the content, the primary brand color #2563EB is used, CTA buttons are present, and the file is at least 5000 characters. Do not remove existing content.
if ${qa_brand} != "ALL_PASS"

source.text: You are a senior QA reviewer performing Verification and Validation. Read index.html and compare it against the requirements in requirements.md. Also review qa-report.md for the automated QA results. Check every acceptance criterion. For each criterion, mark it PASS or FAIL with a brief explanation. Pay special attention to: (1) FAQ accordion actually opens/closes, (2) responsive layout at mobile widths, (3) all 3 pricing tiers present, (4) E2E test pass rate. At the end, if ALL criteria pass, write exactly APPROVED on its own line. If any criteria fail, write REJECTED followed by a numbered list of specific fixes needed. Be thorough but fair — minor cosmetic issues should not cause rejection.

position.text: let review = prompt "You are a senior QA reviewer performing Verification and Validation. Read index.html and compare it against the requirements in requirements.md. Also review qa-report.md for the automated QA results. Check every acceptance criterion. For each criterion, mark it PASS or FAIL with a brief explanation. Pay special attention to: (1) FAQ accordion actually opens/closes, (2) responsive layout at mobile widths, (3) all 3 pricing tiers present, (4) E2E test pass rate. At the end, if ALL criteria pass, write exactly APPROVED on its own line. If any criteria fail, write REJECTED followed by a numbered list of specific fixes needed. Be thorough but fair — minor cosmetic issues should not cause rejection."
let requirements_met = "true"
break

source.text: Based on the review feedback below, generate a concise numbered list of specific fix tasks. Each task should be one actionable item. Review feedback: ${review}. Output ONLY the numbered list.

position.text: let fix_tasks = prompt "Based on the review feedback below, generate a concise numbered list of specific fix tasks. Each task should be one actionable item. Review feedback: ${review}. Output ONLY the numbered list."

body.text: You are a senior frontend developer. Fix this issue in index.html: ${fix}. Read the current file, make the targeted fix, and write it back. Follow brand guidelines (primary

position.text: prompt: You are a senior frontend developer. Fix this issue in index.html: ${fix}. Read the current file, make the targeted fix, and write it back. Follow brand guidelines (primary #2563EB). Do not remove or break existing working sections. Make minimal changes to address the specific issue.
foreach fix in ${fix_tasks}
if ${review} contains "APPROVED"
until requirements_met max 3
run: npx playwright test e2e.spec.js --config=playwright.config.js 2>&1 | tail -15

source.command: node -e \"try{const d=JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('test-results.json','utf8')); const specs=d.suites?.[0]?.specs||[]; const passed=specs.filter(s=>s.tests?.[0]?.results?.[0]?.status==='passed').length; console.log(passed+'/'+specs.length+' passed')}catch(e){console.log('no results')}\"

position.text: let final_e2e = run "node -e \"try{const d=JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('test-results.json','utf8')); const specs=d.suites?.[0]?.specs||[]; const passed=specs.filter(s=>s.tests?.[0]?.results?.[0]?.status==='passed').length; console.log(passed+'/'+specs.length+' passed')}catch(e){console.log('no results')}\""

nodes.command: node -e "const { chromium } = require('playwright'); (async()=>{const b=await chromium.launch({args:['--no-sandbox']}); const ctx=await b.newContext({viewport:{width:1280,height:720}}); const p=await ctx.newPage(); await p.goto('file://'+process.cwd().replace(/\\\\/g,'/')+'/index.html'); await p.waitForLoadState('networkidle'); await p.screenshot({path:'screenshot-final-desktop.png',fullPage:true}); await p.setViewportSize({width:375,height:812}); await p.screenshot({path:'screenshot-final-mobile.png',fullPage:true}); await b.close(); console.log('Final screenshots saved')})()" 2>&1

position.text: run: node -e "const { chromium } = require('playwright'); (async()=>{const b=await chromium.launch({args:['--no-sandbox']}); const ctx=await b.newContext({viewport:{width:1280,height:720}}); const p=await ctx.newPage(); await p.goto('file://'+process.cwd().replace(/\\\\/g,'/')+'/index.html'); await p.waitForLoadState('networkidle'); await p.screenshot({path:'screenshot-final-desktop.png',fullPage:true}); await p.setViewportSize({width:375,height:812}); await p.screenshot({path:'screenshot-final-mobile.png',fullPage:true}); await b.close(); console.log('Final screenshots saved')})()" 2>&1
run: node -e "const h=require('fs').readFileSync('index.html','utf8'); console.log('Final index.html: '+h.length+' chars')"

nodes.text: Summarize the full SDLC + Heavy QA process that was executed. Report on: (1) the key design decisions captured in phases 1-2, (2) how many tasks were planned and implemented in phase 3, (3) E2E test results — how many Playwright tests passed (${final_e2e}), which tests failed if any, and how many retry iterations were needed, (4) Lighthouse audit scores from lighthouse-report.json, (5) code complexity metrics from complexity-report.json, (6) visual snapshots captured (${screenshots_ok}), (7) structural/a11y/seo/brand QA results, (8) whether the review loop approved the result, and (9) final file size and section count. Read qa-report.md for the consolidated results.

position.text: prompt: Summarize the full SDLC + Heavy QA process that was executed. Report on: (1) the key design decisions captured in phases 1-2, (2) how many tasks were planned and implemented in phase 3, (3) E2E test results — how many Playwright tests passed (${final_e2e}), which tests failed if any, and how many retry iterations were needed, (4) Lighthouse audit scores from lighthouse-report.json, (5) code complexity metrics from complexity-report.json, (6) visual snapshots captured (${screenshots_ok}), (7) structural/a11y/seo/brand QA results, (8) whether the review loop approved the result, and (9) final file size and section count. Read qa-report.md for the consolidated results.
